title: Group Therapy in Leesburg, VA | Wildwood Psychotherapy
seo_title: Group Therapy in Leesburg, VA | Wildwood Psychotherapy

blocks.hero_alt: Circle of smooth river stones representing community

blocks.subtitle: Beyond the Individual Journey
blocks.body:
One-on-one therapy offers a deep dive into your personal story, but there's a specific kind of magic that can only happen in the presence of others. Beyond support, our groups are curated, trauma-informed containers designed to help you practice new ways of being in real-time.

Whether you're looking to quiet the noise of perfectionism, process shared trauma, or simply find a space where you don't have to explain yourself, our groups offer a grounded path back to connection.

items.title: Shared Resonance
items.text: There is a specific kind of relief that only happens when you hear someone else voice the exact thought you thought was uniquely yours.

items.title: Relational Laboratory
items.text: Unlike individual therapy, group lets you practice boundaries and authenticity without the pressure to perform in real-time with real people.

items.title: A Different Path
items.text: Groups offer high-level support at a more accessible investment, making consistent healing sustainable.

blocks.title: Now Enrolling: Holding It Together
blocks.subtitle: Current & Upcoming Groups
blocks.body:
A 6-Week Journey for High-Functioning Adults

A small, in-person cohort in Leesburg for those moving past the put-together front to practice regulation and authentic connection.

[View Group Details & Dates →](/holding-it-together)

items.title: Pillar 1
items.subtitle: Intimate Scale
items.text: We keep cohorts small, usually 6-8 people, so the space never feels crowded or overwhelming.

items.subtitle: Intentional Containers
items.title: Pillar 2
items.text: We offer both closed cohorts (for deep, consistent trust), drop-in support, and focused workshops (for specific skill-building). We’ll always be clear about the format before you join.

items.subtitle: Agency + Consent
items.title: Pillar 3
items.text: Your *no* is as respected as your *yes*. You're always in charge of how much you share. No forced vulnerability, ever.

items.title: Step 3
items.text: Join a small, confidential cohort of peers.

items.title: Do I have to share everything?
items.content: Nope. You're always in charge of your own level of vulnerability. Sometimes, just being present and listening can be the thing you need.

items.title: What if I'm too much/not enough?
items.content: This is one of the most common group fears. Our groups are curated to be small and trauma-informed, ensuring everyone has space without anyone feeling overwhelmed. We also hold screening calls to make sure group is an appropriate setting for folks.

items.title: Is this just a vent session?
items.content: While sharing is part of groups, Wildwood groups are structured. We balance emotional processing with practical, concrete tools you can use immediately.

items.title: What if I see someone I know?
items.content: If it's a small world, Loudoun County can feel even smaller sometimes. In our screening call, we check for potential dual relationships to ensure the group remains private. We also establish a firm "Vegas Rule" from minute one: what is said and who is seen in group, stays in group.

items.title: Is this covered by insurance?
items.content: We are not in-network with any insurance provider. However, we provide superbills if you want to submit to your insurance for potential out-of-network reimbursement. We recommend checking with your provider specifically about Group Therapy (CPT Code 90853) coverage.

items.title: How do I know if I'm a good fit?
items.content: Every participant has a 15-minute screening call with [Kaylie](/about), the group facilitator, first. This ensures the group dynamic is balanced and that the program matches your specific goals.

subtitle: Connection & healing in community.
